messages.author: riyaz3000
messages.text: I want to use my existing heltec v3 device i had used on meshtastic that i had built with a the cardkb and gps. If i install the meshcore firmware will i still be able to use the cardkb keyboard and GPS? are the GPIOs and i2c devices supported?
messages.content_html: I want to use my existing heltec v3 device i had used on meshtastic that i had built with a the cardkb and gps. If i install the meshcore firmware will i still be able to use the cardkb keyboard and GPS? are the GPIOs and i2c devices supported?

messages.author: Dakota Sea Witch
messages.text: There's no firmware support currently for the CardKB. I've been working on a meshcore project which includes developing a driver for that keyboard. But even still, I'm not sure what all you'd be able to do on it with the heltec v3 since the firmware doesn't really have support for standalone devices with large screens and an open source GUI. MeshOS and Ripple exist on T-Decks and T-Pagers, but those gui's are not open source, so we're left to develop our own firmwares for this application.
messages.content_html: There's no firmware support currently for the CardKB. I've been working on a meshcore project which includes developing a driver for that keyboard. messages.author: riyaz3000
messages.text: Thanks for replying. I found it useful to reply to messages on my heltec v3 with CardKb with meshtastic. (Can't lie the device is ugly in the printed case I made for it) But was handy for when my kids where watching YouTube on my phone, or when my phone was dead but meshtastic heltec still had battery power left)

messages.author: Dakota Sea Witch
messages.text: Oh yea its super nice to have the standalone capability and not need your phone! Theres just currently no meshcore support in the codebase for enabling any companion radio as a standalone device (aside from the T-Deck line and T-Pager, but that UI code is not open source), nor enabling a configuration for keyboards. This is my one (and perhaps only thus far) gripe about meshcore, we need more standalone device support, given the insane demand on T-Decks and T-Pagers.
